name: ionoscloud
description: A Pulumi package for creating and managing ionoscloud cloud resources.
attribution: This Pulumi package is based on the [`ionoscloud` Terraform Provider](https://github.com/ionos-cloud/terraform-provider-ionoscloud).

nodejs.packageDescription: A Pulumi package for creating and managing ionoscloud cloud resources.

contractNumber.description: To be set only for reseller accounts. Allows to run terraform on a contract number under a reseller account.

endpoint.description: IONOS CLOUD REST API URL. Usually not necessary to be set, SDKs know internally how to route requests to the API.

insecure.description: This field is to be set only for testing purposes. It is not recommended to set this field in production environments.

password.description: IONOS CLOUD password for API operations. If token is provided, token is preferred

retries.deprecationMessage: Timeout is used instead of this functionality

s3AccessKey.description: Access key for IONOS Object Storage operations.

s3Region.description: Region for IONOS Contract Owned Object Storage operations.

s3SecretKey.description: Secret key for IONOS Object Storage operations.

token.description: IONOS CLOUD bearer token for API operations.

username.description: IONOS CLOUD username for API operations. If token is provided, token is preferred

action.description: [string] Specifies the action to be taken when the rule is matched. Possible values: ACCEPTED, REJECTED, ALL. Immutable, forces re-creation.

bucket.description: [string] Specifies the IONOS Object Storage bucket where the flow log data will be stored. The bucket must exist. Immutable, forces re-creation.

direction.description: [string] Specifies the traffic direction pattern. Valid values: INGRESS, EGRESS, BIDIRECTIONAL. Immutable, forces re-creation.

id.description: The resource's unique identifier.

name.description: [string] Specifies the name of the flow log. ⚠️ **Note:** Removing the `flowlog` forces re-creation of the application load balancer resource.

conditions.description: [list] - An array of items in the collection.The action is only performed if each and every condition is met; if no conditions are set, the rule will always be performed.

dropQuery.description: [bool] Default is false; valid only for REDIRECT actions.

location.description: [string] The location for redirecting; mandatory and valid only for REDIRECT actions.

name.description: [string] The unique name of the Application Load Balancer HTTP rule.

responseMessage.description: [string] The response message of the request; mandatory for STATIC action.

statusCode.description: [int] Valid only for REDIRECT and STATIC actions. For REDIRECT actions, default is 301 and possible values are 301, 302, 303, 307, and 308. For STATIC actions, default is 503 and valid range is 200 to 599.

targetGroup.description: [string] The UUID of the target group; mandatory for FORWARD action.

type.description: [string] Type of the Http Rule.

condition.description: [string] Matching rule for the HTTP rule condition attribute; mandatory for HEADER, PATH, QUERY, METHOD, HOST, and COOKIE types; must be null when type is SOURCE_IP.

key.description: [string] Must be null when type is PATH, METHOD, HOST, or SOURCE_IP. Key can only be set when type is COOKIES, HEADER, or QUERY.

negate.description: [bool] Specifies whether the condition is negated or not; the default is False.

type.description: [string] Type of the Http Rule condition.

value.description: [string] Mandatory for conditions CONTAINS, EQUALS, MATCHES, STARTS_WITH, ENDS_WITH; must be null when condition is EXISTS; should be a valid CIDR if provided and if type is SOURCE_IP.

action.description: Specifies the action to be taken when the rule is matched. Possible values: ACCEPTED, REJECTED, ALL. Immutable, forces re-creation.

bucket.description: Specifies the IONOS Object Storage bucket where the flow log data will be stored. The bucket must exist. Immutable, forces re-creation.

direction.description: Specifies the traffic direction pattern. Valid values: INGRESS, EGRESS, BIDIRECTIONAL. Immutable, forces re-creation.

id.description: ID of the application load balancer you want to search for.

name.description: Name of an existing application load balancer that you want to search for. Search by name is case-insensitive. The whole resource name is required if `partialMatch` parameter is not set to true.

conditions.description: An array of items in the collection.The action is only performed if each and every condition is met; if no conditions are set, the rule will always be performed.

dropQuery.description: Default is false; valid only for REDIRECT actions.

location.description: The location for redirecting; mandatory and valid only for REDIRECT actions.

name.description: Name of an existing application load balancer that you want to search for. Search by name is case-insensitive. The whole resource name is required if `partialMatch` parameter is not set to true.

responseMessage.description: The response message of the request; mandatory for STATIC action.

statusCode.description: Valid only for REDIRECT and STATIC actions. For REDIRECT actions, default is 301 and possible values are 301, 302, 303, 307, and 308. For STATIC actions, default is 503 and valid range is 200 to 599.

targetGroup.description: The UUID of the target group; mandatory for FORWARD action.

type.description: Type of the Http Rule condition.

condition.description: Matching rule for the HTTP rule condition attribute; mandatory for HEADER, PATH, QUERY, METHOD, HOST, and COOKIE types; must be null when type is SOURCE_IP.

key.description: Must be null when type is PATH, METHOD, HOST, or SOURCE_IP. Key can only be set when type is COOKIES, HEADER, or QUERY.

negate.description: Specifies whether the condition is negated or not; the default is False.

type.description: Type of the Http Rule condition.

value.description: Mandatory for conditions CONTAINS, EQUALS, MATCHES, STARTS_WITH, ENDS_WITH; must be null when condition is EXISTS; should be a valid CIDR if provided and if type is SOURCE_IP.

metric.description: [string] The Metric that should trigger the scaling actions. Metric values are checked at fixed intervals. Possible values: `INSTANCE_CPU_UTILIZATION_AVERAGE`, `INSTANCE_NETWORK_IN_BYTES`, `INSTANCE_NETWORK_IN_PACKETS`, `INSTANCE_NETWORK_OUT_BYTES`, `INSTANCE_NETWORK_OUT_PACKETS`

range.description: [string] Defines the time range, for which the samples will be aggregated. Default is 120s. *Note that when you set it to values like 5m the API will automatically transform it in PT5M, so the plan will show you a diff in state that should be ignored.*

scaleInAction.description: [list] Specifies the action to take when the `scaleInThreshold` is exceeded. Hereby, scaling in is always about removing VMs that are currently associated with this autoscaling group. Default termination policy is OLDEST_SERVER_FIRST.

scaleInThreshold.description: [int] A lower threshold on the value of `metric`. Will be used with `less than` (<) operator. Exceeding this will start a Scale-In Action as specified by the `scaleInAction` property. The value must have a higher minimum delta to the `scaleOutThreshold` depending on the `metric` to avoid competitive actions at the same time.

scaleOutAction.description: [list] Specifies the action to take when the `scaleOutThreshold` is exceeded. Hereby, scaling out is always about adding new VMs to this autoscaling group.

scaleOutThreshold.description: [int] The upper threshold for the value of the `metric`. Used with the `greater than` (>) operator. A scale-out action is triggered when this value is exceeded, specified by the `scaleOutAction` property. The value must have a lower minimum delta to the `scaleInThreshold`, depending on the metric, to avoid competing for actions simultaneously. If `properties.policy.unit=TOTAL`, a value >= 40 must be chosen.

unit.description: [string] Units of the applied Metric. Possible values are: `PER_HOUR`, `PER_MINUTE`, `PER_SECOND`, `TOTAL`.

amount.description: [int] When `amountType == ABSOLUTE`, this is the number of VMs removed in one step. When `amountType == PERCENTAGE`, this is a percentage value, which will be applied to the autoscaling group's current `targetReplicaCount` in order to derive the number of VMs that will be removed in one step. There will always be at least one VM removed. For SCALE_IN operation new volumes are NOT deleted after the server deletion.

amountType.description: [string] The type for the given amount. Possible values are: `ABSOLUTE`, `PERCENTAGE`.

cooldownPeriod.description: [string] Minimum time to pass after this Scaling action has started, until the next Scaling action will be started. Additionally, if a Scaling action is currently in progress, no second Scaling action will be started for the same autoscaling group. Instead, the Metric will be re-evaluated after the current Scaling action is completed (either successfully or with failures). This is validated with a minimum value of 2 minutes and a maximum of 24 hours currently. Default value is 5 minutes if not given. *Note that when you set it to values like 5m the API will automatically transform it in PT5M, so the plan will show you a diff in state that should be ignored.*

deleteVolumes.description: [bool] If set to `true`, when deleting a replica during scale in, any attached volume will also be deleted. When set to `false`, all volumes remain in the datacenter and must be deleted manually. Note that every scale-out creates new volumes. When they are not deleted, they will eventually use all of your contracts resource limits. At this point, scaling out would not be possible anymore.

terminationPolicyType.description: [string] The type of the termination policy for the autoscaling group so that a specific pattern is followed for Scaling-In replicas. Default termination policy is `OLDEST_SERVER_FIRST`. Possible values are: `OLDEST_SERVER_FIRST`, `NEWEST_SERVER_FIRST`, `RANDOM`

amount.description: [int] When `amountType=ABSOLUTE` specifies the absolute number of VMs that are added. The value must be between 1 to 10. `amountType=PERCENTAGE` specifies the percentage value that is applied to the current number of replicas of the VM Auto Scaling Group. The value must be between 1 to 200. At least one VM is always added.

amountType.description: [string] The type for the given amount. Possible values are: `ABSOLUTE`, `PERCENTAGE`.

cooldownPeriod.description: [string] Minimum time to pass after this Scaling action has started, until the next Scaling action will be started. Additionally, if a Scaling action is currently in progress, no second Scaling action will be started for the same autoscaling group. Instead, the Metric will be re-evaluated after the current Scaling action is completed (either successfully or with failures). This is validated with a minimum value of 2 minutes and a maximum of 24 hours currently. Default value is 5 minutes if not given. *Note that when you set it to values like 5m the API will automatically transform it in PT5M, so the plan will show you a diff in state that should be ignored.*

availabilityZone.description: [string] The zone where the VMs are created using this configuration. Possible values are: `AUTO`, `ZONE_1`, `ZONE_2`.

cores.description: [int] The total number of cores for the VMs.

cpuFamily.description: [string] CPU family for the VMs created using this configuration. If null, the VM will be created with the default CPU family for the assigned location. Possible values are: `INTEL_SKYLAKE`, `INTEL_XEON`.

nics.description: Set of NICs associated with this Replica.

ram.description: [int] The amount of memory for the VMs in MB, e.g. 2048. Size must be specified in multiples of 256 MB with a minimum of 256 MB; however, if you set ramHotPlug to TRUE then you must use a minimum of 1024 MB. If you set the RAM size more than 240GB, then ramHotPlug will be set to FALSE and can not be set to TRUE unless RAM size not set to less than 240GB.

volumes.description: [list] List of volumes associated with this Replica.

dhcp.description: Dhcp flag for this replica Nic. This is an optional attribute with default value of 'true' if not given in the request payload or given as null.

firewallActive.description: Activate or deactivate the firewall. By default, an active firewall without any defined rules will block all incoming network traffic except for the firewall rules that explicitly allows certain protocols, IP addresses and ports.

firewallRules.description: List of all firewall rules for the specified NIC.

firewallType.description: The type of firewall rules that will be allowed on the NIC. If not specified, the default INGRESS value is used.

flowLogs.description: List of all flow logs for the specified NIC.

lan.description: Lan ID for this replica Nic.

name.description: [string] User-defined name for the Autoscaling Group.

targetGroup.description: In order to link VM to ALB, target group must be provided.

icmpCode.description: Sets the allowed code (from 0 to 254) when ICMP protocol is selected. The value 'null' allows all codes.

icmpType.description: Sets the allowed type (from 0 to 254) if the protocol ICMP is selected. The value 'null' allows all types.

name.description: [string] User-defined name for the Autoscaling Group.

portRangeEnd.description: Sets the end range of the allowed port (from 1 to 65535) if the protocol TCP or UDP is selected. The value 'null' for 'port_range_start' and 'port_range_end' allows all ports.

portRangeStart.description: Sets the initial range of the allowed port (from 1 to 65535) if the protocol TCP or UDP is selected. The value 'null' for 'port_range_start' and 'port_range_end' allows all ports.

protocol.description: The protocol for the rule. The property cannot be modified after its creation (not allowed in update requests).

sourceIp.description: Only traffic originating from the respective IPv4 address is permitted. The value 'null' allows traffic from any IP address.

sourceMac.description: Only traffic originating from the respective MAC address is permitted. Valid format: 'aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff'. The value 'null' allows traffic from any MAC address.

targetIp.description: If the target NIC has multiple IP addresses, only the traffic directed to the respective IP address of the NIC is allowed. The value 'null' allows traffic to any target IP address.

type.description: The firewall rule type. If not specified, the default value 'INGRESS' is used.

action.description: Specifies the traffic direction pattern. Valid values: ACCEPTED, REJECTED, ALL. Immutable, forces re-recreation of the nic resource.

bucket.description: The bucket name of an existing IONOS Object Storage bucket. Immutable, forces re-recreation of the nic resource.

direction.description: Specifies the traffic direction pattern. Valid values: INGRESS, EGRESS, BIDIRECTIONAL. Immutable, forces re-recreation of the nic resource.

id.description: The resource's unique identifier.

name.description: [string] User-defined name for the Autoscaling Group.

port.description: The port for the target group.

targetGroupId.description: The ID of the target group.

weight.description: The weight for the target group.

backupUnitId.description: [string] The uuid of the Backup Unit that user has access to. The property is immutable and is only allowed to be set on a new volume creation. It is mandatory to provide either `public image` or `imageAlias` in conjunction with this property.

bootOrder.description:
[string] Determines whether the volume will be used as a boot volume. Set to NONE, the volume will not be used as boot volume. Set to PRIMARY, the volume will be used as boot volume and set to AUTO will delegate the decision to the provisioning engine to decide whether to use the volume as boot volume.
Notice that exactly one volume can be set to PRIMARY or all of them set to AUTO.

bus.description: [string] The bus type of the volume. Default setting is `VIRTIO`. The bus type `IDE` is also supported.

image.description: [string] The image installed on the volume. Only the UUID of the image is presently supported.

imageAlias.description: [string] The image installed on the volume. Must be an `imageAlias` as specified via the images API. Note that one of `image` or `imageAlias` must be set, but not both.

imagePassword.description: [string] Image password for this replica volume.

name.description: [string] Name for this replica volume.

size.description: [int] Name for this replica volume.

sshKeys.description: List of ssh keys, supports values or paths to files. Cannot be changed at update.

type.description: [string] Storage Type for this replica volume. Possible values: `SSD`, `HDD`, `SSD_STANDARD` or `SSD_PREMIUM`.

userData.description: [string] User-data (Cloud Init) for this replica volume. Make sure you provide a Cloud Init compatible image in conjunction with this parameter.

id.description: The unique ID of the server.

prefix.description: [string] The prefix of the routing rule.

scheme.description: [string] The scheme of the routing rule.

upstream.description: [map] - A map of properties for the rule

caching.description: [bool] Enable or disable caching. If enabled, the CDN will cache the responses from the upstream host. Subsequent requests for the same resource will be served from the cache.

host.description: [string] The upstream host that handles the requests if not already cached. This host will be protected by the WAF if the option is enabled.

rateLimitClass.description: [string] Rate limit class that will be applied to limit the number of incoming requests per IP.

sniMode.description: [string] The SNI (Server Name Indication) mode of the upstream. It supports two modes: 1) `distribution`: for outgoing connections to the upstream host, the CDN requires the upstream host to present a valid certificate that matches the configured domain of the CDN distribution; 2) `origin`: for outgoing connections to the upstream host, the CDN requires the upstream host to present a valid certificate that matches the configured upstream/origin hostname.

waf.description: [bool] Enable or disable WAF to protect the upstream host.

location.description: The physical location of the connectable datacenter

name.description: [string] The name of the cross-connection.

datacenterId.description: The id of the cross-connected datacenter

datacenterName.description: The name of the cross-connected datacenter

lanName.description: The name of the cross-connected LAN

location.description: The location of the cross-connected datacenter

dhcpv6.description: Indicates whether this NIC receives an IPv6 address through DHCP.

firewall.description: Firewall rules created in the server resource. The rules can also be created as separate resources outside the server resource

ips.description: Collection of IP addresses assigned to a nic. Explicitly assigned public IPs need to come from reserved IP blocks, Passing value null or empty array will assign an IP address automatically.

ipv6CidrBlock.description: IPv6 CIDR block assigned to the NIC.

ipv6Ips.description: Collection for IPv6 addresses assigned to a nic. Explicitly assigned IPv6 addresses need to come from inside the IPv6 CIDR block assigned to the nic.

name.description: [string] The name of the server.

securityGroupsIds.description: The list of Security Group IDs for the resource.

name.description: [string] The name of the server.

availabilityZone.description: [string] The availability zone in which the server should exist. This property is immutable.

backupUnitId.description: The uuid of the Backup Unit that user has access to. The property is immutable and is only allowed to be set on a new volume creation. It is mandatory to provide either 'public image' or 'imageAlias' in conjunction with this property.

bootServer.description: The UUID of the attached server.

exposeSerial.description: If set to `true` will expose the serial id of the disk attached to the server. If set to `false` will not expose the serial id. Some operating systems or software solutions require the serial id to be exposed to work properly. Exposing the serial can influence licensed software (e.g. Windows) behavior

userData.description: The cloud-init configuration for the volume as base64 encoded string. The property is immutable and is only allowed to be set on a new volume creation. maxRam.description: The maximum number of RAM in MB

dhcpv6.description: Indicates whether this NIC receives an IPv6 address through DHCP.

firewall.description: Firewall rules created in the server resource. The rules can also be created as separate resources outside the server resource

ips.description: Collection of IP addresses assigned to a nic. Explicitly assigned public IPs need to come from reserved IP blocks, Passing value null or empty array will assign an IP address automatically.

ipv6CidrBlock.description: IPv6 CIDR block assigned to the NIC.

ipv6Ips.description: Collection for IPv6 addresses assigned to a nic. Explicitly assigned IPv6 addresses need to come from inside the IPv6 CIDR block assigned to the nic.

bootServer.description: The UUID of the attached server.

exposeSerial.description: If set to `true` will expose the serial id of the disk attached to the server. If set to `false` will not expose the serial id. gatewayIps.description: [list] Collection of gateway IP addresses of the NAT gateway. Will be auto-generated if not provided. Should ideally be an IP belonging to the same subnet as the LAN.

id.description: [int] Id for the LAN connected to the NAT gateway.

end.description: [int] Target port range end associated with the NAT gateway rule.

start.description: [int] Target port range start associated with the NAT gateway rule.

action.description: Specifies the action to be taken when the rule is matched. Possible values: ACCEPTED, REJECTED, ALL. Immutable, update forces re-creation.

bucket.description: Specifies the IONOS Object Storage bucket where the flow log data will be stored. The bucket must exist. Immutable, update forces re-creation.

direction.description: Specifies the traffic direction pattern. Valid values: INGRESS, EGRESS, BIDIRECTIONAL. Immutable, update forces re-creation.

This property is immutable.

availabilityZone.description: [string] The availability zone in which the server should exist. E.g: `AUTO`, `ZONE_1`, `ZONE_2`. This property is immutable.

exposeSerial.description: If set to `true` will expose the serial id of the disk attached to the server. If set to `false` will not expose the serial id. name.description: [string] The name of the server.

requireLegacyBios.description: Indicates if the image requires the legacy BIOS for compatibility or specific needs.

size.description: The size of the volume in GB.

sshKeyPaths.description: [list] List of absolute paths to files containing a public SSH key that will be injected into IONOS CLOUD provided Linux images. Also accepts ssh keys directly. Required for IONOS CLOUD Linux images. Required if `imagePassword` is not provided. Does not support `~` expansion to homedir in the given path. This property is immutable.
sshKeyPaths.deprecationMessage: Please use sshKeyPath under server level

sshKeys.description: [list] Immutable List of absolute or relative paths to files containing public SSH key that will be injected into IONOS CLOUD provided Linux images. Also accepts ssh keys directly. Public SSH keys are set on the image as authorized keys for appropriate SSH login to the instance using the corresponding private key. This field may only be set in creation requests. When reading, it always returns null. SSH keys are only supported if a public Linux image is used for the volume creation. Does not support `~` expansion to homedir in the given path.
sshKeys.deprecationMessage: Please use sshKeys under server level

userData.description: The cloud-init configuration for the volume as base64 encoded string. The property is immutable and is only allowed to be set on a new volume creation. It is mandatory to provide either 'public image' or 'imageAlias' that has cloud-init compatibility in conjunction with this property.

checkInterval.description: [int] The interval in milliseconds between consecutive health checks; default is 2000.

checkTimeout.description: [int] The maximum time in milliseconds to wait for a target to respond to a check. For target VMs with 'Check Interval' set, the lesser of the two  values is used once the TCP connection is established.

retries.description: [int] The maximum number of attempts to reconnect to a target after a connection failure. Valid range is 0 to 65535, and default is three reconnection.

method.description: [string] The method for the HTTP health check.

path.description: [string] The path (destination URL) for the HTTP health check request; the default is /.

response.description: [string] The response returned by the request, depending on the match type.

healthCheckEnabled.description: [bool] Makes the target available only if it accepts periodic health check TCP connection attempts; when turned off, the target is considered always available. The health check only consists of a connection attempt to the address and port of the target. Default is True.

ip.description: [string] The IP of the balanced target VM.

maintenanceEnabled.description: [bool] Maintenance mode prevents the target from receiving balanced traffic.

port.description: [int] The port of the balanced target service; valid range is 1 to 65535.

proxyProtocol.description: [string] The proxy protocol version. Accepted values are `none`, `v1`, `v2`, `v2ssl`. If unspecified, the default value of `none` is used.

weight.description: [int] Traffic is distributed in proportion to target weight, relative to the combined weight of all targets. A target with higher weight receives a greater share of traffic. Valid range is 0 to 256 and default is 1; targets with weight of 0 do not participate in load balancing but still accept persistent connections. It is best use values in the middle of the range to leave room for later adjustments.

dhcpv6.description: Indicates whether this NIC receives an IPv6 address through DHCP.

ipv6Ips.description: Collection for IPv6 addresses assigned to a nic. Explicitly assigned IPv6 addresses need to come from inside the IPv6 CIDR block assigned to the nic.

datacenterId.description: The ID of the datacenter where the WireGuard Gateway is located.

ipv4Cidr.description: The VPN Gateway IPv4 address in CIDR notation. This is the private gateway address for LAN clients to route traffic over the VPN Gateway, this should be within the subnet already assigned to the LAN.

ipv6Cidr.description: The VPN Gateway IPv6 address in CIDR notation. This is the private gateway address for LAN clients to route traffic over the VPN Gateway, this should be within the subnet already assigned to the LAN.

lanId.description: The ID of the LAN where the WireGuard Gateway is connected.

dayOfTheWeek.description: The name of the week day.

time.description: Start of the maintenance window in UTC time.

host.description: Hostname or IPV4 address that the WireGuard Server will connect to.

port.description: Port that the WireGuard Server will connect to. Default: 51820

provider.description:
The provider type for the ionoscloud package. By default, resources use package-wide configuration
settings, however an explicit `Provider` instance may be created and passed during resource
construction to achieve fine-grained programmatic control over provider settings. See the
[documentation](https://www.pulumi.com/docs/reference/programming-model/#providers) for more information.

contractNumber.description: To be set only for reseller accounts. Allows to run terraform on a contract number under a reseller account.

endpoint.description: IONOS CLOUD REST API URL. Usually not necessary to be set, SDKs know internally how to route requests to the API.

insecure.description: This field is to be set only for testing purposes. It is not recommended to set this field in production environments.

password.description: IONOS CLOUD password for API operations. If token is provided, token is preferred

retries.deprecationMessage: Timeout is used instead of this functionality

s3AccessKey.description: Access key for IONOS Object Storage operations.

s3Region.description: Region for IONOS Contract Owned Object Storage operations.

s3SecretKey.description: Secret key for IONOS Object Storage operations.

token.description: IONOS CLOUD bearer token for API operations.

username.description: IONOS CLOUD username for API operations. If token is provided, token is preferred

centralLogging.description: [bool] Turn logging on and off for this product. Default value is 'false'.

datacenterId.description: [string] ID of the datacenter.

flowlog.description: [list] Only 1 flow log can be configured. Only the name field can change as part of an update. Flow logs holistically capture network information such as source and destination IP addresses, source and destination ports, number of packets, amount of bytes, the start and end time of the recording, and the type of protocol – and log the extent to which your instances are being accessed.

ips.description: [set] Collection of the Application Load Balancer IP addresses. (Inbound and outbound) IPs of the listenerLan are customer-reserved public IPs for the public Load Balancers, and private IPs for the private Load Balancers.

lbPrivateIps.description: [set] Collection of private IP addresses with the subnet mask of the Application Load Balancer. IPs must contain valid a subnet mask. If no IP is provided, the system will generate an IP with /24 subnet.

loggingFormat.description: Specifies the format of the logs.

name.description: [string] The name of the Application Load Balancer.

targetLan.description: [int] ID of the balanced private target LAN (outbound).

maxReplicaCount.description: [int] The maximum value for the number of replicas on a VM Auto Scaling Group. Must be >= 0 and <= 200. Will be enforced for both automatic and manual changes.

minReplicaCount.description: [int] The minimum value for the number of replicas on a VM Auto Scaling Group. Must be >= 0 and <= 200. Will be enforced for both automatic and manual changes.

name.description: [string] User-defined name for the Autoscaling Group.

policy.description: [List] Specifies the behavior of this Autoscaling Group. A policy consists of Triggers and Actions, whereby an Action is some kind of automated behavior, and a Trigger is defined by the circumstances under which the Action is triggered. Currently, two separate Actions, namely Scaling In and Out are supported, triggered through Thresholds defined on a given Metric.

bootDeviceId.description:
[string] The ID of a bootable device such as a volume or an image data source. If this field is omitted from the configuration, the VM will be restarted with no primary boot device, and it will enter the PXE shell for network booting. 
***Note***: If the network booting process started by the PXE shell fails, the VM will still boot into the image of the attached storage as a fallback. This behavior imitates the "Boot from Network" option from [DCD](https://dcd.ionos.com/).

allowReplace.description: [bool] When set to true, allows the update of immutable fields by first destroying and then re-creating the server. ⚠️ **_Warning: `allowReplace` - lets you update immutable fields, but it first destroys and then re-creates the server in order to do it. This field should be used with care, understanding the risks._** > **⚠ WARNING** > > Image_name under volume level is deprecated, please use imageName under server level > **⚠ WARNING** > > For creating a **CUBE** server, you can not set `volume.size` argument. >

name.description: [string] The name of the firewall rule.

portRangeEnd.description: [int] Defines the end range of the allowed port (from 1 to 65534) if the protocol TCP or UDP is chosen. Leave portRangeStart and portRangeEnd null to allow all ports.

portRangeStart.description: [int] Defines the start range of the allowed port (from 1 to 65534) if protocol TCP or UDP is chosen. Leave portRangeStart and portRangeEnd null to allow all ports.

protocol.description: [string] The protocol for the rule: TCP, UDP, ICMP, ANY. Property cannot be modified after creation (disallowed in update requests).

sourceIp.description: [string] Only traffic originating from the respective IPv4 address is allowed. Value null allows all source IPs.

sourceMac.description: [string] Only traffic originating from the respective MAC address is allowed. Valid format: aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff. Value null allows all source MAC address. Valid format: aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff.

targetIp.description: [string] In case the target NIC has multiple IP addresses, only traffic directed to the respective IP address of the NIC is allowed. Value null allows all target IPs.

type.description: [string] The type of firewall rule. If is not specified, it will take the default value INGRESS.

datacenterId.description: [string] The ID of a Virtual Data Center.

ip.description: [string] The reserved IP address to be used in the IP failover group.

lanId.description: [string] The ID of a LAN.

nicuuid.description: [string] The ID of a NIC. > **⚠ WARNING:** Do not modify the IP for an IP failover group (that was provisioned via Terraform) > using the DCD, the API or other means because it may lead to unexpected behavior. If you provisioned > an IP failover group using Terraform, please use only Terraform in order to manage the created > IP failover group. > **⚠ WARNING:** For creating multiple IP failover groups at the same time, you can use one of the > following options: 1. Create multiple IP failover groups resources and use `dependsOn` meta-argument to specify the order of creation, for example: ```example resource "ionoscloud_ipfailover" "firstexample" { datacenter_id = "datacenter ID" lan_id = "LAN ID" ip = "IP address" nicuuid = "NIC UUID" } resource "ionoscloud_ipfailover" "secondexample" { depends_on = [ ionoscloud_ipfailover.firstexample ] datacenter_id = "datacenter ID" lan_id = "LAN ID" ip = "IP address" nicuuid = "NIC UUID" } ``` 2. Define the resources as presented above, but without using the `dependsOn` meta-argument and run the apply command using `-parallelism=1` as presented below: ```shell pulumi up -parallelism=1 ```

datacenterId.description: [string] The ID of a Virtual Data Center.

ipv4CidrBlock.description: [String] For public LANs this property is null, for private LANs it contains the private IPv4 CIDR range. This property is a read only property.

ipv6CidrBlock.description: Contains the LAN's /64 IPv6 CIDR block if this LAN is IPv6 enabled. 'AUTO' will result in enabling this LAN for IPv6 and automatically assign a /64 IPv6 CIDR block to this LAN. If you specify your own IPv6 CIDR block then you must provide a unique /64 block, which is inside the IPv6 CIDR block of the virtual datacenter and unique inside all LANs from this virtual datacenter.

location.description: The location of the resource. This field should be used only if you are also using a file configuration and should not be configured otherwise.

name.description: [string] The name of the LAN.

pcc.description: [String] The unique id of a `ionoscloud.compute.Crossconnect` resource, in order. It needs to be ensured that IP addresses of the NICs of all LANs connected to a given Cross Connect is not duplicated and belongs to the same subnet range

public.description: [Boolean] Indicates if the LAN faces the public Internet (true) or not (false).

name.description: [string] Name of the NAT gateway rule.

protocol.description: [string] Protocol of the NAT gateway rule. Defaults to ALL. If protocol is 'ICMP' then targetPortRange start and end cannot be set.

publicIp.description: [string] Public IP address of the NAT gateway rule. Specifies the address used for masking outgoing packets source address field. Should be one of the customer reserved IP address already configured on the NAT gateway resource.

sourceSubnet.description: [string] Source subnet of the NAT gateway rule. For SNAT rules it specifies which packets this translation rule applies to based on the packets source IP address.

targetPortRange.description: Target port range of the NAT gateway rule. For SNAT rules it specifies which packets this translation rule applies to based on destination port. If none is provided, rule will match any port.

targetSubnet.description: [string] Target or destination subnet of the NAT gateway rule. For SNAT rules it specifies which packets this translation rule applies to based on the packets destination IP address. If none is provided, rule will match any address.

type.description: [string] Type of the NAT gateway rule.

datacenterId.description: [string] The ID of a Virtual Data Center.

deviceNumber.description: The Logical Unit Number (LUN) of the storage volume. Null if this NIC was created from CloudAPI and no DCD changes were done on the Datacenter.

dhcp.description: [Boolean] Indicates if the NIC should get an IP address using DHCP (true) or not (false).

dhcpv6.description: [Boolean] Indicates if the NIC should get an IPv6 address using DHCP (true) or not (false).

firewallActive.description: [Boolean] If this resource is set to true and is nested under a server resource firewall, with open SSH port, resource must be nested under the NIC.

firewallType.description: [String] The type of firewall rules that will be allowed on the NIC. If it is not specified it will take the default value INGRESS

flowlog.description: Only 1 flow log can be configured. Only the name field can change as part of an update. Flow logs holistically capture network information such as source and destination IP addresses, source and destination ports, number of packets, amount of bytes, the start and end time of the recording, and the type of protocol – and log the extent to which your instances are being accessed.

ips.description: [list] Collection of IP addresses assigned to a NIC. Explicitly assigned public IPs need to come from reserved IP blocks, Passing value null or empty array will assign an IP address automatically.

ipv6CidrBlock.description: Automatically assigned /80 IPv6 CIDR block if the NIC is connected to an IPv6 enabled LAN. You can also specify an /80 IPv6 CIDR block for the NIC on your own, which must be inside the /64 IPv6 CIDR block of the LAN and unique.

ipv6Ips.description: [list] Collection of IPv6 addresses assigned to a NIC. Explicitly assigned public IPs need to come from the NIC's Ipv6 CIDR block, Passing value null or empty array will assign an IPv6 address automatically from the NIC's CIDR block.

lan.description: [integer] The LAN ID the NIC will sit on.

location.description: The location of the resource. This field should be used only if you are also using a file configuration and should not be configured otherwise.

mac.description: The MAC address of the NIC. Can be set on creation only. If not set, one will be assigned automatically by the API. Immutable, update forces re-creation.

name.description: [string] The name of the LAN.

pciSlot.description: The PCI slot number of the Nic.

securityGroupsIds.description: The list of Security Group IDs for the resource. ⚠️ **Note:**: Removing the `flowlog` forces re-creation of the NIC resource.

bootImage.description: [string] The image or snapshot UUID / name. May also be an image alias. It is required if `licenceType` is not provided.

cores.description: (Computed)[integer] Number of server CPU cores.

cpuFamily.description: [string] CPU architecture on which server gets provisioned; not all CPU architectures are available in all datacenter regions; available CPU architectures can be retrieved from the datacenter resource. E.g.: "INTEL_SKYLAKE" or "INTEL_XEON".

datacenterId.description: [string] The ID of a Virtual Data Center.

hostname.description: (Computed)[string] The hostname of the resource. Allowed characters are a-z, 0-9 and - (minus). Hostname should not start with minus and should not be longer than 63 characters. If no value provided explicitly, it will be populated with the name of the server

imageName.description: [string] The name, ID or alias of the image. May also be a snapshot ID. It is required if `licenceType` is not provided. Attribute is immutable.

imagePassword.description: [string] Required if `sshKeyPath` is not provided.

inlineVolumeIds.description: A list with the IDs for the volumes that are defined inside the server resource.

labels.description: [set] A label can be seen as an object with only two required fields: `key` and `value`, both of the `string` type. Please check the example presented above to see how a `label` can be used in the plan. A server can have multiple labels.

location.description: The location of the resource. This field should be used only if you are also using a file configuration and should not be configured otherwise.

name.description: [string] The name of the server.

nicMultiQueue.description: [bool] Activate or deactivate the Multi Queue feature on all NICs of the server. This feature is beneficial to enable when the NICs are experiencing performance issues (e.g. low throughput). Toggling this feature will also initiate a restart of the server. If the specified value is `true`, the feature will be activated; if it is not specified or set to `false`, the feature will be deactivated. The feature cannot be activated for `CUBE` servers. ⚠️ **_Warning: `allowReplace` - lets you update immutable fields, but it first destroys and then re-creates the server in order to do it. This field should be used with care, understanding the risks._** > **⚠ WARNING** > > Image_name under volume level is deprecated, please use imageName under server level > sshKeyPath and sshKeys fields are immutable. > **⚠ WARNING** > > If you want to create a **CUBE** server, you have to provide the `templateUuid`. In this case you can not set `cores`, `ram` and `volume.size` arguments, these being mutually exclusive with `templateUuid`. > > In all the other cases (**ENTERPRISE** servers) you have to provide values for `cores`, `ram` and `volume size`.

ram.description: (Computed)[integer] The amount of memory for the server in MB.

securityGroupsIds.description: The list of Security Group IDs for the

sshKeyPaths.description: [list] List of absolute paths to files containing a public SSH key that will be injected into IONOS CLOUD provided Linux images. Also accepts ssh keys directly. Required for IONOS CLOUD Linux images. Required if `imagePassword` is not provided. Does not support `~` expansion to homedir in the given path. This property is immutable.
sshKeyPaths.deprecationMessage: Will be renamed to sshKeys in the future, to allow users to set both the ssh key path or directly the ssh key

sshKeys.description: [list] Immutable List of absolute or relative paths to files containing public SSH key that will be injected into IONOS CLOUD provided Linux images. Also accepts ssh keys directly. Public SSH keys are set on the image as authorized keys for appropriate SSH login to the instance using the corresponding private key. This field may only be set in creation requests. When reading, it always returns null. SSH keys are only supported if a public Linux image is used for the volume creation. Does not support `~` expansion to homedir in the given path.

sharePrivilege.description: [Boolean] The group has permission to share this resource. ⚠️ **Note:** There is a limitation due to which the creation of several shares at the same time leads to an error. To avoid this, `parallelism=1` can be used when running `pulumi up` command in order to create the resources in a sequential manner. Another solution involves the usage of `dependsOn` attributes inside the `ionoscloud.compute.Share` resource to enforce the sequential creation of the shares.

active.description: [Boolean] Indicates if the user is active

administrator.description: [Boolean] Indicates if the user has administrative rights. Administrators do not need to be managed in groups, as they automatically have access to all resources associated with the contract.

email.description: [string] An e-mail address for the user.

firstName.description: [string] A first name for the user.

forceSecAuth.description: [Boolean] Indicates if secure (two-factor) authentication should be forced for the user (true) or not (false).

groupIds.description: [Set] The groups that this user will be a member of **NOTE:** Group_ids field cannot be used at the same time with userIds field in group resource. Trying to add the same user to the same group in both ways in the same plan will result in a cyclic dependency error. **NOTE:** `passwordWo` requires Teraform 1.11 or higher.

lastName.description: [string] A last name for the user.

password.description: A password for the user. If you are using terraform 1.11 or higher, you can use `passwordWo` instead of `password` to avoid storing the password in the state file.

passwordWo.description: **NOTE:** This field is write-only and its value will not be updated in state as part of read operations. user password. This value is always marked as sensitive in the plan output, regardless of `type`. Additionally, `write-only` values are never stored to state. `passwordWoVersion` can be used to trigger an update and is required with this argument. In Terraform CLI version 0.15 and later, this may require additional configuration handling for certain scenarios. For more information, see the Terraform v0.15 Upgrade Guide.

passwordWoVersion.description: Used together with `passwordWo` to trigger an update. Increment this value when an update to the `passwordWo` is required.

imagePassword.description: [string] The password for the image.

inlineVolumeIds.description: A list with the IDs for the volumes that are defined inside the server resource.

labels.description: A label can be seen as an object with only two required fields: `key` and `value`, both of the `string` type. Please check the example presented above to see how a `label` can be used in the plan. A server can have multiple labels.

location.description: The location of the resource. This field should be used only if you are also using a file configuration and should not be configured otherwise.

name.description: [string] The name of the server.

nicMultiQueue.description: [bool] Activate or deactivate the Multi Queue feature on all NICs of the server. This feature is beneficial to enable when the NICs are experiencing performance issues (e.g. low throughput). Toggling this feature will also initiate a restart of the server. If the specified value is `true`, the feature will be activated; if it is not specified or set to `false`, the feature will be deactivated. > **⚠ WARNING** > > sshKeys field is immutable.

ram.description: [integer] The amount of memory for the server in MB.

securityGroupsIds.description: The list of Security Group IDs for the resource.

sshKeys.description: [list] Immutable List of absolute or relative paths to files containing public SSH key that will be injected into IONOS CLOUD provided Linux images. Also accepts ssh keys directly. Public SSH keys are set on the image as authorized keys for appropriate SSH login to the instance using the corresponding private key. This field may only be set in creation requests. When reading, it always returns null. SSH keys are only supported if a public Linux image is used for the volume creation. Does not support `~` expansion to homedir in the given path.

discVirtioHotUnplug.description: [string] Is capable of Virt-IO drive hot unplug (no reboot required). This works only for non-Windows virtual Machines.

diskType.description: [string] The volume type: HDD or SSD. This property is immutable.

exposeSerial.description: (Computed) [boolean] Defaults to `false` if not previously set by the image used to create the volume. If set to `true` will expose the serial id of the disk attached to the server. If set to `false` will not expose the serial id. Some operating systems or software solutions require the serial id to be exposed to work properly. Exposing the serial can influence licensed software (e.g. Windows) behavior

ramHotPlug.description: [string] Is capable of memory hot plug (no reboot required)

ionoscloud:dbaas/inMemoryDBReplicaSet:In.description: Manages a [DBaaS InMemoryDB Replica Set](https://docs.ionos.com/cloud/databases/in-memory-db/overview) Replica Set. ## Import Resource DBaaS InMemoryDB Replica Set can be imported using the `replicasetId` and the `location`, separated by `:`, e.g: ```sh terraform import ionoscloud_inmemorydb_replicaset.example location:replicaSet uuid ```

connections.description: [object] The network connection for your replica set. Only one connection is allowed. Updates to the value of the fields force the replica set to be re-created.

credentials.description: [object] Credentials for the InMemoryDB replicaset, only one type of password can be used since they are mutually exclusive. These values are used to create the initial InMemoryDB user, updating any of these will force recreation of the replica set resource.

displayName.description: [string] The human-readable name of your replica set.

dnsName.description: [string] The DNS name pointing to your replica set. Will be used to connect to the active/standalone instance. > **⚠ NOTE:** `IONOS_API_URL_INMEMORYDB` can be used to set a custom API URL for the resource. `location` field needs to be empty, otherwise it will override the custom API URL. Setting `endpoint` or `IONOS_API_URL` does not have any effect.

evictionPolicy.description: [string] The eviction policy for the replica set, possible values are:

initialSnapshotId.description: [string] The ID of a snapshot to restore the replica set from. If set, the replica set will be created from the snapshot.

location.description: [string] The location of your replica set. Updates to the value of the field force the replica set to be re-created. If this is not set and if no value is provided for the `IONOS_API_URL` env var, the default `location` will be: `de/fra`, other available locations are: `de/fra/2`, `de/txl`, `es/vit`, `gb/bhx`, `gb/lhr`, `us/ewr`, `us/las`, `us/mci`, `fr/par`

maintenanceWindow.description: (Computed) A weekly 4 hour-long window, during which maintenance might occur.

persistenceMode.description:
[string] Specifies How and If data is persisted, possible values are:
* `None` - Data is inMemory only and will not be persisted. Useful for cache only applications.
* `AOF` - (Append Only File) AOF persistence logs every write operation received by the server. These operations can then be replayed again at server startup, reconstructing the original dataset. Commands are logged using the same format as the InMemoryDB protocol itself.
* `RDB` - RDB persistence performs snapshots of the current in memory state.
* `RDB_AOF` - Both RDB and AOF persistence are enabled.

replicas.description: [int] The total number of replicas in the replica set (one active and n-1 passive). In case of a standalone instance, the value is 1. In all other cases, the value is > 1. The replicas will not be available as read replicas, they are only standby for a failure of the active instance.

resources.description: [object] The resources of the individual replicas.

version.description: [string] The InMemoryDB version of your replica set.

connections.description: [object] The network connection for your replica set. Only one connection is allowed. Updates to the value of the fields force the replica set to be re-created.

credentials.description: [object] Credentials for the InMemoryDB replicaset, only one type of password can be used since they are mutually exclusive. These values are used to create the initial InMemoryDB user, updating any of these will force recreation of the replica set resource.

displayName.description: [string] The human-readable name of your replica set.

evictionPolicy.description: [string] The eviction policy for the replica set, possible values are:

initialSnapshotId.description: [string] The ID of a snapshot to restore the replica set from. If set, the replica set will be created from the snapshot.

location.description: [string] The location of your replica set. Updates to the value of the field force the replica set to be re-created. If this is not set and if no value is provided for the `IONOS_API_URL` env var, the default `location` will be: `de/fra`, other available locations are: `de/fra/2`, `de/txl`, `es/vit`, `gb/bhx`, `gb/lhr`, `us/ewr`, `us/las`, `us/mci`, `fr/par`

maintenanceWindow.description: (Computed) A weekly 4 hour-long window, during which maintenance might occur.

persistenceMode.description:
[string] Specifies How and If data is persisted, possible values are:
* `None` - Data is inMemory only and will not be persisted. Useful for cache only applications.
* `AOF` - (Append Only File) AOF persistence logs every write operation received by the server. These operations can then be replayed again at server startup, reconstructing the original dataset. Commands are logged using the same format as the InMemoryDB protocol itself.
* `RDB` - RDB persistence performs snapshots of the current in memory state.
* `RDB_AOF` - Both RDB and AOF persistence are enabled.

replicas.description: [int] The total number of replicas in the replica set (one active and n-1 passive). In case of a standalone instance, the value is 1. In all other cases, the value is > 1. The replicas will not be available as read replicas, they are only standby for a failure of the active instance.

backup.description: Properties configuring the backup of the cluster. Immutable, change forces re-creation of the cluster.

connections.description: The network connection for your cluster. Only one connection is allowed.

ionoscloud:dbaas/pSQLClusterV2:PSQLClust.description: Manages a DBaaS PostgreSQL v2 Cluster. #### Required * `id` (String) The UUID of the cluster. * `location` (String) The location of the cluster (e.g. `de/txl`). --- A cluster can also be imported using the format `location:cluster_id`: ```sh $ pulumi import ionoscloud:dbaas/pSQLClusterV2:PSQLClusterV2 mycluster de/txl:cluster_uuid ```

description.description: [string] Human-readable description for the cluster.

dnsName.description: [string] The DNS name used to access the cluster.

instances.description: [object] The instance configuration for the PostgreSQL cluster.

location.description: [string] The location of the PostgreSQL cluster. This is used for routing to the regional API endpoint. Changing this value will destroy the existing cluster and create a new one in the specified location. Available locations: `de/fra`, `de/fra/2`, `de/txl`, `es/vit`, `fr/par`, `gb/bhx`, `gb/lhr`, `us/ewr`, `us/las`, `us/mci`.

replicationMode.description: [string] Replication mode across the instances. Possible values: `ASYNCHRONOUS`, `STRICTLY_SYNCHRONOUS`.

version.description: [string] The PostgreSQL version of the cluster.

ionoscloud:k8s/nodePool:NodePool.description: Manages a **Managed Kubernetes Node Pool**, part of a managed Kubernetes cluster on IONOS CLOUD. ## Import A Kubernetes Node Pool resource can be imported using its Kubernetes cluster's uuid as well as its own UUID, both of which you can retrieve from the cloud API: `resource id`, e.g.: ```sh $ pulumi import ionoscloud:k8s/nodePool:NodePool demo k8s_cluster_uuid/k8s_nodepool_id ``` This can be helpful when you want to import kubernetes node pools which you have already created manually or using other means, outside of terraform, towards the goal of managing them via Terraform ⚠️ **_Warning: **During a maintenance window, k8s can update your `k8sVersion` if the old one reaches end of life. This upgrade will not be shown in the plan, as we prevent terraform from doing a downgrade, as downgrading `k8sVersion` is not supported._** ⚠️ **_Warning: **If you are upgrading from v5.x.x to v6.x.x**: You have to modify you plan for lans to match the new structure, by putting the ids from the old slice in lans.id fields. This is not backwards compatible._**

allowReplace.description: [bool] When set to true, allows the update of immutable fields by first destroying and then re-creating the node pool. ⚠️ **_Warning: `allowReplace` - lets you update immutable fields, but it first destroys and then re-creates the node pool in order to do it. Set the field to true only if you know what you are doing. This will cause a downtime for all pods on that nodepool. Consider adding multiple nodepools and update one after the other for downtime free nodepool upgrade._** Immutable fields list: name, cpu_family, availability_zone, cores_count, ram_size, storage_size, storage_type. ⚠️ **Note**: Be careful when using `autoScaling` since the number of nodes can change. Because of that, when running `pulumi preview`, Terraform will think that an update is required (since `nodeCount` from the `tf` plan will be different from the number of nodes set by the scheduler). To avoid that, you can use:

autoScaling.description: [string] Whether the Node Pool should autoscale. For more details, please check the API documentation

availabilityZone.description: [string] - The desired Compute availability zone - See the API documentation for more information. *This attribute is immutable*.

coresCount.description: [int] - The CPU cores count for each node of the node pool. *This attribute is immutable*.

cpuFamily.description: [string] The desired CPU Family - See the API documentation for more information. *This attribute is immutable*.

datacenterId.description: [string] A Datacenter's UUID

k8sVersion.description: [string] The desired Kubernetes Version. For supported values, please check the API documentation. Downgrades are not supported. The provider will ignore downgrades of patch level.

lans.description: [list] A list of numeric LAN id's you want this node pool to be part of. For more details, please check the API documentation, as well as the example above

location.description: The location of the resource. This field should be used only if you are also using a file configuration and should not be configured otherwise.

maintenanceWindow.description: See the **maintenance_window** section in the example above

name.description: [string] The name of the Kubernetes Cluster. *This attribute is immutable*.

nodeCount.description: [int] - The desired number of nodes in the node pool

publicIps.description: [list] A list of public IPs associated with the node pool; must have at least `nodeCount + 1` elements

ramSize.description: [int] - The desired amount of RAM, in MB. *This attribute is immutable*.

serverType.description: [string] The server type for the compute engine - See the API documentation for more information. Possible values: `DedicatedCore`, `VCPU`

storageSize.description: [int] - The size of the volume in GB. The size should be greater than 10GB. *This attribute is immutable*.

storageType.description: [string] - The desired storage type - SSD/HDD. *This attribute is immutable*.

name.description: [string] Name of the Kafka Cluster.

numberOfPartitions.description:
[int] The number of partitions of the topic. Partitions allow for parallel
processing of messages. The partition count must be greater than or equal to the replication factor. Minimum value: 1.
Default value: 3.

replicationFactor.description:
[int] The number of replicas of the topic. The replication factor determines how many
copies of the topic are stored on different brokers. The replication factor must be less than or equal to the number
of brokers in the Kafka Cluster. Minimum value: 1. Default value: 3.

retentionTime.description:
[int] This configuration controls the maximum time we will retain a log before we will
discard old log segments to free up space. This represents an SLA on how soon consumers must read their data. If set
to -1, no time limit is applied. Default value: 604800000.

segmentBytes.description:
[int] This configuration controls the segment file size for the log. Retention and
cleaning is always done a file at a time so a larger segment size means fewer files but less granular control over
retention. Default value: 1073741824.

uid.description: The user ID that will own the exported directory. If not set, **anonymous** (`512`) will be used.

forceDestroy.description: [bool] Default is `false`.By setting forceDestroy to true, you instruct Terraform to delete the bucket and all its contents during the terraform destroy process. This is particularly useful when dealing with buckets that contain objects, as it allows for automatic cleanup without requiring the manual deletion of objects beforehand. If forceDestroy is not set or is set to false, Terraform will refuse to delete a bucket that still contains objects. You must manually empty the bucket before Terraform can remove it.There is a significant risk of accidental data loss when using this attribute, as it irreversibly deletes all contents of the bucket. It's crucial to ensure that the bucket does not contain critical data before using force_destroy.

region.description: [string] Specifies the Region where the bucket will be created. Available regions are: `eu-central-3`, `eu-central-4`, `us-central-1`. Can be used only if the region is the same as the global region (set using `IONOS_S3_REGION` env var or `s3Region` provider attribute) or if the global region is unset. For using multiple different regions, please check the `Working with multiple regions/locations` section presented here.

ionoscloud:objectstorage/bucketObject:Bu.description:
Manages **IONOS Object Storage Objects** on IONOS CLOUD.


## Import

Resource Object can be imported using the `bucket name` and `object key`

```sh
$ pulumi import ionoscloud:objectstorage/bucketObject:BucketObject example example/object
```

blockPublicPolicy.description: [bool] Blocks public access to the bucket via bucket policies. Bucket policies that grant public access will not be allowed.

ignorePublicAcls.description: [bool] Instructs the system to ignore any ACLs that grant public access. Even if ACLs are set to allow public access, they will be disregarded.

restrictPublicBuckets.description: [bool] Restricts access to buckets that have public policies. Buckets with policies that grant public access will have their access restricted.

inputs.description: A collection of arguments for invoking getBalancer.

datacenterId.description: Datacenter's UUID.

id.description: ID of the application load balancer you want to search for.

name.description: Name of an existing application load balancer that you want to search for. Search by name is case-insensitive. The whole resource name is required if `partialMatch` parameter is not set to true.

partialMatch.description: Whether partial matching is allowed or not when using name argument. Default value is false. `datacenterId` and either `name` or `id` must be provided. If none, or both of `name` and `id` are provided, the datasource will return an error.

outputs.description: A collection of values returned by getBalancer.

centralLogging.description: Turn logging on and off for this product. Default value is 'false'.

flowlogs.description: Only 1 flow log can be configured. Only the name field can change as part of an update. Flow logs holistically capture network information such as source and destination IP addresses, source and destination ports, number of packets, amount of bytes, the start and end time of the recording, and the type of protocol – and log the extent to which your instances are being accessed.

id.description: Id of the application load balancer.

ips.description: Collection of the Application Load Balancer IP addresses. (Inbound and outbound) IPs of the listenerLan are customer-reserved public IPs for the public Load Balancers, and private IPs for the private Load Balancers.

lbPrivateIps.description: Collection of private IP addresses with the subnet mask of the Application Load Balancer. IPs must contain valid a subnet mask. If no IP is provided, the system will generate an IP with /24 subnet.

name.description: Specifies the name of the flow log.

targetLan.description: ID of the balanced private target LAN (outbound).

inputs.description: A collection of arguments for invoking getForwardingRule.

datacenterId.description: Datacenter's UUID.

id.description: ID of the application load balancer you want to search for.

location.description: The location for redirecting; mandatory and valid only for REDIRECT actions.

name.description: Name of an existing application load balancer that you want to search for. Search by name is case-insensitive. The whole resource name is required if `partialMatch` parameter is not set to true.

partialMatch.description: Whether partial matching is allowed or not when using name argument. Default value is false. Both `datacenterId` and `applicationLoadbalancerId` and either `name` or `id` must be provided. If none, or both of `name` and `id` are provided, the datasource will return an error.

outputs.description: A collection of values returned by getForwardingRule.

clientTimeout.description:
The maximum time in milliseconds to wait for the client to acknowledge or send data; default is 50,000 (50 seconds).
- `server certificates` - Array of items in that collection.

httpRules.description: Array of items in that collection

id.description: Id of Application Load Balancer Forwarding Rule

listenerPort.description: Listening (inbound) port number; valid range is 1 to 65535.

location.description: The location for redirecting; mandatory and valid only for REDIRECT actions.

name.description: The unique name of the Application Load Balancer HTTP rule.

inputs.description: A collection of arguments for invoking getGroup.

id.description: Id of an existing Autoscaling Group that you want to search for.

name.description: Name of an existing Autoscaling Group that you want to search for. Either `name` or `id` must be provided. If none or both are provided, the datasource will return an error.

outputs.description: A collection of values returned by getGroup.

id.description: Unique identifier for the resource

location.description: Location of the datacenter. This location is the same as the one from the selected template.

maxReplicaCount.description: Maximum replica count value for `targetReplicaCount`. Will be enforced for both automatic and manual changes.

minReplicaCount.description: Minimum replica count value for `targetReplicaCount`. Will be enforced for both automatic and manual changes.

name.description: The name of the Autoscaling Group.

policies.description: Specifies the behavior of this Autoscaling Group. A policy consists of Triggers and Actions, whereby an Action is some kind of automated behavior, and a Trigger is defined by the circumstances under which the Action is triggered. Currently, two separate Actions, namely Scaling In and Out are supported, triggered through Thresholds defined on a given Metric.

inputs.description: A collection of arguments for invoking getServers.

groupId.description: The unique ID of the autoscaling group. `groupId` must be provided. If it is not provided, the datasource will return an error.

outputs.description: A collection of values returned by getServers.

id.description: The provider-assigned unique ID for this managed resource.

inputs.description: A collection of arguments for invoking getDistribution.

domain.description: Domain of an existing distribution that you want to search for. Search by domain is case-insensitive. The whole resource domain is required if `partialMatch` parameter is not set to true.

id.description: ID of the distribution you want to search for.

partialMatch.description: Whether partial matching is allowed or not when using domain argument. Default value is false. Either `domain` or `id` must be provided. If none, or both of `domain` and `id` are provided, the datasource will return an error.

outputs.description: A collection of values returned by getDistribution.

certificateId.description: The ID of the certificate to use for the distribution. You can create certificates with the certificate resource.

publicEndpointV4.description: IP of the distribution, it has to be included on the domain DNS Zone as A record.

publicEndpointV6.description: IP of the distribution, it has to be included on the domain DNS Zone as AAAA record.

routingRules.description: The routing rules for the distribution.

inputs.description: A collection of arguments for invoking getAutoCertificate.

id.description: [string] ID of the auto-certificate you want to search for. Either `name` or `id` must be provided. If none, or both are provided, the datasource will return an error.

location.description: [string] The location of the auto-certificate. Available locations: `de/fra`, `de/fra/2`.

name.description: [string] Name of an existing auto-certificate that you want to search for.

outputs.description: A collection of values returned by getAutoCertificate.

commonName.description: [string] The common name (DNS) of the certificate to issue. The common name needs to be part of a zone in IONOS CLOUD DNS.

keyAlgorithm.description: [string] The key algorithm used to generate the certificate.

lastIssuedCertificateId.description: [string] The ID of the last certificate that was issued.

subjectAlternativeNames.description: [list][string] Optional additional names to be added to the issued certificate. The additional names needs to be part of a zone in IONOS CLOUD DNS.

inputs.description: A collection of arguments for invoking getAutoCertificateProvider.

id.description: [string] ID of the provider you want to search for. Either `name` or `id` must be provided. If none, or both are provided, the datasource will return an error.

location.description: [string] The location of the provider. Available locations: `de/fra`, `de/fra/2`

name.description: [string] Name of an existing provider that you want to search for.

outputs.description: A collection of values returned by getAutoCertificateProvider.

email.description: [string] The email address of the certificate requester.

server.description: [string] The URL of the certificate provider.

inputs.description: A collection of arguments for invoking getCertificate.

id.description: ID of the certificate you want to search for. Either `name` or `id` must be provided, or both. If none are provided, the datasource will return an error.

name.description: Name of an existing certificate that you want to search for.

outputs.description: A collection of values returned by getCertificate.

id.description: The id of the certificate.

name.description: The name of the certificate.

inputs.description: A collection of arguments for invoking getBackupUnit.

id.description: ID of the backup unit you want to search for. Either `name` or `id` must be provided. If none, or both are provided, the datasource will return an error.

name.description: Name of an existing backup unit that you want to search for.

outputs.description: A collection of values returned by getBackupUnit.

email.description: The e-mail address you want assigned to the backup unit.

id.description: The id of the Backup Unit.

login.description: The login associated with the backup unit. Derived from the contract number.

name.description: The name of the Backup Unit.

inputs.description: A collection of arguments for invoking getCrossconnect.

id.description: ID of the cross connect you want to search for. Either `name` or `id` must be provided. If none, or both are provided, the datasource will return an error.

location.description: The physical location of the connectable datacenter

name.description: Name of an existing cross connect that you want to search for.

outputs.description: A collection of values returned by getCrossconnect.

connectableDatacenters.description: Lists datacenters that can be joined to this cross connect

description.description: Description of cross connect

id.description: The UUID of the connectable datacenter

location.description: The physical location of the connectable datacenter

name.description: The name of the connectable datacenter

peers.description: Lists LAN's joined to this cross connect

inputs.description: A collection of arguments for invoking getCubeServer.

datacenterId.description: Datacenter's UUID.

id.description: ID of the server you want to search for. `datacenterId` and either `name` or `id` must be provided. If none, or both of `name` and `id` are provided, the datasource will return an error.

location.description: Location of that image/snapshot

name.description: Name of an existing server that you want to search for.

outputs.description: A collection of values returned by getCubeServer.

availabilityZone.description: The availability zone in which the volume should exist

datacenterId.description: The id of the datacenter

hostname.description: The hostname of the server

id.description: Id of the firewall rule

location.description: Location of that image/snapshot

name.description: Name of the firewall rule

securityGroupsIds.description: The list of Security Group IDs for the resource.

templateUuid.description: The UUID of the template for creating a CUBE server; the available templates for CUBE servers can be found on the templates resource

vmState.description: Status of the virtual Machine

inputs.description: A collection of arguments for invoking getDatacenter.

id.description: Id of an existing Virtual Data Center that you want to search for.

location.description: Id of the existing Virtual Data Center's location. Available locations: `de/fra`, `us/las`, `us/ewr`, `de/txl`, `gb/lhr`, `gb/bhx`, `es/vit`, `fr/par`, `us/mci`, `de/fra/2` Either `name`, `location` or `id` must be provided. If none, the datasource will return an error.

name.description: Name of an existing Virtual Data Center that you want to search for.

outputs.description: A collection of values returned by getDatacenter.

cpuArchitectures.description: Array of features and CPU families available in a location

description.description: Description for the Virtual Data Center

features.description: List of features supported by the location this data center is part of

id.description: UUID of the Virtual Data Center

location.description: The regional location where the Virtual Data Center is created

name.description: The name of the Virtual Data Center

secAuthProtection.description: Boolean value representing if the data center requires extra protection e.g. two factor protection

version.description: The version of that Data Center. Gets incremented with every change

inputs.description: A collection of arguments for invoking getFirewall.

datacenterId.description: The Virtual Data Center ID.

id.description: ID of the firewall rule you want to search for.

name.description: Name of an existing firewall rule that you want to search for.

nicId.description: The NIC ID. Either `name` or `id` must be provided. If none, or both are provided, the datasource will return an error.

outputs.description: A collection of values returned by getFirewall.

icmpCode.description: Defines the allowed code (from 0 to 254) if protocol ICMP is chosen.

icmpType.description: Defines the allowed type (from 0 to 254) if the protocol ICMP is chosen.

id.description: The id of the firewall rule.

name.description: The name of the firewall rule.

portRangeEnd.description: Defines the end range of the allowed port (from 1 to 65534) if the protocol TCP or UDP is chosen.

portRangeStart.description: Defines the start range of the allowed port (from 1 to 65534) if protocol TCP or UDP is chosen.

protocol.description: The protocol for the rule: TCP, UDP, ICMP, ANY. This property is immutable.

Searching for a server using filter name and value `test`, will find all servers that have `test` in the name. Available locations: `de/fra`, `de/fra/2`, `de/txl`, `es/vit`, `gb/bhx`, `gb/lhr`, `us/ewr`, `us/las`, `us/mci`, `fr/par` > **Note:** Either `id` or `displayName` must be provided. credentials.description: [object] Credentials for the InMemoryDB replicaset, only one type of password can be used since they are mutually exclusive. It includes:

dnsName.description: [string] The DNS name pointing to your replica set. Will be used to connect to the active/standalone instance.

evictionPolicy.description: [string] The eviction policy for the replica set, possible values are:

maintenanceWindows.description: A weekly 4 hour-long window, during which maintenance might occur. It includes:

persistenceMode.description:
[string] Specifies How and If data is persisted, possible values are:
* `None` - Data is inMemory only and will not be persisted. Useful for cache only applications.
* `AOF` - (Append Only File) AOF persistence logs every write operation received by the server. These operations can then be replayed again at server startup, reconstructing the original dataset. Commands are logged using the same format as the InMemoryDB protocol itself.
* `RDB` - RDB persistence performs snapshots of the current in memory state.
* `RDB_AOF` - Both, RDB and AOF persistence are enabled.

replicas.description: [int] The total number of replicas in the replica set (one active and n-1 passive). In case of a standalone instance, the value is 1. In all other cases, the value is > 1. The replicas will not be available as read replicas, they are only standby for a failure of the active instance.

inputs.description: A collection of arguments for invoking getClusters.

filters.description: One or more property name - value pairs to be used in filtering the cluster list by the specified attributes. You can use most of the top level fields from the k8sCluster resource **except** those containing other nested structures such as `maintenanceWindow` or `config`. **NOTE:** Filtering uses partial matching for all types of values. Searching for a cluster using `name:testCluster` will find all clusters who have the `testCluster` substring in their name. This also applies to values for properties that would normally be boolean or numerical.

outputs.description: A collection of values returned by getClusters.

clusters.description: list of Kubernetes clusters that match the provided filters. The elements of this list are structurally identical to the `k8sCluster` datasource, which is limited to retrieving only 1 cluster in a single query.

entries.description:
indicates the number of clusters found and added to the list after the query has been performed with the specified filters.
For a full reference of all the attributes returned, check out documentation
