clusterId.description: ID of the Kafka Cluster that the topic belongs to.

id.description: ID of an existing Kafka Cluster Topic that you want to search for.

location.description:
The location of the Kafka Cluster Topic. Must be the same as the location of the Kafka
Cluster. Possible values: `de/fra`, `de/fra/2`, `de/txl`, `fr/par`, `es/vit`, `gb/lhr`, `gb/bhx`, `us/las`, `us/mci`, `us/ewr`.

name.description: Name of an existing Kafka Cluster Topic that you want to search for.

outputs.description: A collection of values returned by getTopic.

clusterId.description: The id of the Kafka Cluster that the topic belongs to.

id.description: UUID of the Kafka Cluster Topic.

name.description: The name of the Kafka Cluster Topic.

numberOfPartitions.description: The number of partitions of the topic. Partitions allow for parallel processing of messages.

replicationFactor.description:
The number of replicas of the topic. The replication factor determines how many copies of the
topic are stored on different brokers.

retentionTime.description:
This configuration controls the maximum time we will retain a log before we will discard old log
segments to free up space. This represents an SLA on how soon consumers must read their data. If set to -1, no time
limit is applied.

segmentBytes.description: This configuration controls the segment file size for the log. Retention and cleaning is always done a file at a time so a larger segment size means fewer files but less granular control over retention.

inputs.description: A collection of arguments for invoking getPipeline.

grafanaAddress.description: The Grafana address is where user can access their logs, create dashboards, and set up alerts.

httpAddress.description: The HTTP address of the pipeline. This is the address to which logs are sent using the HTTP protocol.

logs.description: [list] Pipeline logs, a list that contains elements with the following structure:

name.description: The name of the Logging pipeline.

tcpAddress.description: The TCP address of the pipeline. This is the address to which logs are sent using the TCP protocol.

inputs.description: A collection of arguments for invoking getCluster.

location.description: The location where the Network File Storage cluster is located. Available locations: `de/fra`, `de/fra/2`, `de/txl`, `fr/par`, `gb/lhr`, `es/vit`, `us/las`, `us/ewr`, `us/mci`.

partialMatch.description: Whether partial matching is allowed or not when using the name filter. Defaults to `false`.

connections.description: A list of connections for the Network File Storage cluster. You can specify only one connection. Each connection supports the following:

location.description: The location where the Network File Storage cluster is located.

name.description: The name of the Network File Storage cluster.

nfs.description: The NFS configuration for the Network File Storage cluster. Each NFS configuration supports the following:

size.description: The size of the Network File Storage cluster. Note that the cluster size cannot be reduced after provisioning. This value determines the billing fees.

sizeUnit.description: The unit of the `size` attribute. Possible values: `TiB`, `GiB`.

inputs.description: A collection of arguments for invoking getShare.

clientGroups.description: The groups of clients are the systems connecting to the Network File Storage cluster. Each client group supports the following:

clusterId.description: The ID of the Network File Storage cluster.

gid.description: The group ID that will own the exported directory. If not set, **anonymous** (`512`) will be used.

id.description: ID of the Network File Storage share.

location.description: The location where the Network File Storage share is located. Available locations: `de/fra`, `de/fra/2`, `de/txl`, `fr/par`, `gb/lhr`, `es/vit`, `us/las`, `us/ewr`, `us/mci`.

name.description: Name of the Network File Storage share.

partialMatch.description: Whether partial matching is allowed or not when using the name filter. Defaults to `false`.

quota.description: The quota in MiB for the export. The quota can restrict the amount of data that can be stored within the export. The quota can be disabled using `0`.

uid.description: The user ID that will own the exported directory. If not set, **anonymous** (`512`) will be used.

inputs.description: A collection of arguments for invoking getBalancer.

datacenterId.description: Datacenter's UUID.

id.description: ID of the network load balancer you want to search for. `datacenterId` and either `name` or `id` must be provided. If none, or both of `name` and `id` are provided, the datasource will return an error.

centralLogging.description: Turn logging on and off for this product. Default value is 'false'.

flowlogs.description: Only 1 flow log can be configured. Only the name field can change as part of an update. Flow logs holistically capture network information such as source and destination IP addresses, source and destination ports, number of packets, amount of bytes, the start and end time of the recording, and the type of protocol – and log the extent to which your instances are being accessed.

id.description: Id of that Network Load Balancer

ips.description: Collection of IP addresses of the Network Load Balancer. (inbound and outbound) IP of the listenerLan must be a customer reserved IP for the public load balancer and private IP for the private load balancer.

lbPrivateIps.description: Collection of private IP addresses with subnet mask of the Network Load Balancer. IPs must contain valid subnet mask. If user will not provide any IP then the system will generate one IP with /24 subnet.

name.description: Specifies the name of the flow log.

targetLan.description: Id of the balanced private target LAN. (outbound)
